title: mistake unsafe command preToolUse allowlist
purpose: G5.7 defines mistake unsafe command pretooluse allowlist for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake.
scope: Owns `G5.7` only; siblings under `G5` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §9 — Branch G — Verification & quality plane (anti-mistake)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#9-branch-g-verification-quality-plane-anti-mistake).
behavior_steps: When `strict_hitl` is off and pursuit reaches HLD, DD, or manifest gates, the conductor reads the configured self-gate mode from state—checklist-only, automated LLM reviewer, or second economy reviewer—before treating a middle gate as cleared without H3.

In checklist-only mode, gate clearance requires every checklist row to map to an evidence file or verify command that [G2.1](G2.1-goal-verify-command-state-pack.md) can audit; the conductor must not advance `next_action` on conductor prose alone.

When reviewer mode is active, a genius-tier [B2.5](B2.5-reviewer-bugbot-security-risk-triggers.md) subagent evaluates the artifact under scoped `allowed_reads` and records pass/fail in state before design phases resume.

Until an ADR resolves this open decision, pursuit defaults to checklist plus machine-checkable evidence paths so middle gates stay fail-closed under [A5.2](A5.2-continue-not-approval-self-gate-h1-h3-only.md).
If self-gate mode is undefined, reviewer output conflicts with evidence, or checklist items lack proof, pursuit stops at H2—middle design gates never waive silently.

Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Flaky test â€” escalation S4 once, then H2 with evidence log; no silent retry loop.
Edge case `G5.7` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `G5.7`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Extend verify-router for goal-level suite invocation.
Wire CI: validate-workflow checks goal block when pursuit.mode=goal_autopilot.
Validate `G5.7` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `G5.7` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `G5.7`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: G5.7 defines mistake unsafe command pretooluse allowlist for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake. In practice, the system when `strict_hitl` is off and pursuit reaches hld, dd, or manifest gates, the conductor reads the configured self-gate mode from state—checklist-only, automated llm reviewer, or second economy reviewer—before treating a middle gate as cleared without h3, then in checklist-only mode, gate clearance requires every checklist row to map to an evidence file or verify command that [g2.1](g2.1-goal-verify-command-state-pack.md) can audit; the conductor must not advance `next_action` on conductor prose alone.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-g 2026-06-28 -->

Unsafe commands are shell invocations outside role allowlists—destructive rm, credential exfiltration patterns, or network calls forbidden by pack policy. Controls: preToolUse hooks in `.cursor/hooks.json`, per-role tool permissions from template-pack ([B5.2](B5.2-role-to-pipeline-id-skills-tool-permissions.md)), and conductor rejection of worker-proposed commands not on the task card.

Tool-operator and verifier workers run bounded literal commands only; economy search must not escalate to unapproved system commands. Hook deny is fail-closed with journal note, not silent strip.
